example.name: single_blueprint_to_business_object_catalog
example.description: Generate business object JSON artifacts from a single blueprint document.
example.context: The user has one business blueprint document that describes a business domain and wants JSON business object files plus a catalog for later packaging.

workflow.step: Inspect the relevant source folder under business-blueprints
Read the blueprint document together with comparable repository examples
Extract business entities, fields, enums, and references

example.expected_outcome: The repository contains business object JSON files under business-objects/generated/[context]/, a context catalog under business-objects/catalog/[context].catalog.json, an updated master catalog at business-objects/catalog/master.catalog.json, and packaging remains intentionally deferred.

example.name: multiple_documents_same_context
example.description: Generate a unified set of business objects from several related blueprint documents.
example.context: The user provides multiple documents that all belong to the same business domain and expects deduplicated output artifacts.

workflow.step: Read all selected blueprint files together
Merge overlapping business concepts into a single normalized model
Resolve duplicates and conflicting field descriptions conservatively
Generate one consistent business object set for the shared context under business-objects/generated/[context]/
Capture unresolved conflicts in business-objects/reports/[context].parse-report.md if needed

example.expected_outcome: Related documents produce one coherent set of business object artifacts instead of duplicate or contradictory JSON files.

example.name: handoff_to_packaging_mode
example.description: Hand off cleanly to packaging after business object generation is complete.
example.context: The user confirms that generated business object JSON artifacts are ready to be packaged into a toolkit.
example.handoff_pattern: <switch_mode> <mode_slug>baw-package-manager</mode_slug> <reason>Business object JSON catalog is ready for toolkit packaging.</reason> </switch_mode>
example.important: The switch happens only after generation work in this mode is complete and only when the user is ready to move to packaging.

example.name: avoid_out_of_scope_packaging
example.description: Preserve boundaries when the user asks for packaging during generation analysis.
example.context: The user starts with blueprint parsing but then asks to package immediately before generation artifacts are complete.
example.correct_response: Finish or clarify the generation task first. Do not run packaging commands in this mode. If the user wants packaging after generation readiness is established, switch to baw-package-manager.
